novel_features.name: Bulk export to your bike computer
novel_features.description: Export many routes or trips to GPX, TCX, CSV, or KML files in one command — with cue sheets included, ready to side-load onto a Garmin, Wahoo, or Hammerhead. The bulk export Ride with GPS itself won't do.
novel_features.rationale: Synthesizes files from the local track_points/course_points mirror so a whole library exports in one pass; --native streams RideWithGPS's own render including device-native FIT. No competitor ships bulk export.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user wants routes onto a bike computer or rides as files on disk — one command instead of clicking export per route in the web app.

novel_features.name: Routes from an event
novel_features.description: List the routes attached to an event (group ride, gran fondo, brevet) and optionally export them straight to files for your bike computer.
novel_features.rationale: Pulls the event's embedded routes via the legacy event endpoint and reuses the export path — the API's v1 event response doesn't surface routes, and no other tool connects events to device-ready files.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a user has signed up for an organized ride and wants that event's official routes on their head unit.

novel_features.name: Gear mileage & maintenance
novel_features.description: Per-bike accumulated mileage from your logged rides, plus maintenance-due flags against wear thresholds.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the synced gear and trips tables locally to sum distance per bike — data that is dashboard-only in RideWithGPS and queryable nowhere else.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to answer 'how many miles since my last chain swap' or 'which bike needs service' without a spreadsheet.

novel_features.name: Training stats
novel_features.description: Time-windowed distance, elevation, and moving-time totals with activity-type breakdowns over your logged rides.
novel_features.rationale: Aggregates the local trips mirror by week/month/year — there is no API aggregation endpoint, so this only works against a local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this for 'how far did I ride this month' or 'how much have I climbed this year' — period totals, not single efforts.

novel_features.name: Personal records
novel_features.description: All-time best efforts — longest ride, most climbing, fastest average speed, biggest power — from your trip metrics.
novel_features.rationale: Sorts the local trips mirror on track-point-derived metrics to surface maxima RideWithGPS never exposes (it has no segments or PR feature).
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this for 'what's my longest ride' or 'my biggest climbing day' — top-N extremes, not period sums.

novel_features.name: Route dedup
novel_features.description: Find near-duplicate routes by distance and start/end geometry, and optionally delete the extras.
novel_features.rationale: Clusters routes by rounded distance + start/end coords + bounding box in the local store — a cross-row comparison no single API call can do.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to clean a route library full of near-identical versions of the same loop.

novel_features.name: Library audit
novel_features.description: Flag routes that are stale, private, or incomplete (missing name, description, or distance) — catalog hygiene across your library.
novel_features.rationale: Runs predicate checks over the synced routes in SQLite — catalog hygiene across hundreds of assets that the web UI makes you click through one at a time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this before an event season to find routes that are stale, private when they should be shared, or missing details.

narrative.headline: Your whole Ride with GPS library, offline — bulk export, gear mileage, and ride analytics no other tool ships.
narrative.value_prop: A fast, scriptable, agent-native CLI for Ride with GPS with a local SQLite mirror of your routes and trips. Bulk-export everything (with cue sheets) to GPX/TCX/CSV for your bike computer with 'export', pull an organized ride's routes with 'event-routes', track per-bike mileage with 'gear', see this month's climbing with 'stats', surface all-time bests with 'records', and clean a junk-drawer library with 'dedup' and 'audit' — none of which the web app or any existing tool does.
narrative.auth_narrative: Ride with GPS uses two credentials: a client API key and a per-user auth token, sent as the x-rwgps-api-key and x-rwgps-auth-token headers. Create an API client under your account's developers tab for the API key, then set RIDEWITHGPS_API_KEY and RIDEWITHGPS_AUTH_TOKEN. To get your auth token from your email and password, run 'auth-tokens --user-email <you> --user-password <pw>', which returns it. The v1 API does not accept browser cookies; run 'doctor' to verify.

quickstart.comment: health check — confirms credentials and API reachability without writing anything
mirror your routes and trips into the local SQLite store
this month's distance, elevation, and moving time from the local mirror
bulk-export every route to GPX — the thing the web app won't do
per-bike mileage rolled up from your logged rides

troubleshoots.fix: Set RIDEWITHGPS_API_KEY and RIDEWITHGPS_AUTH_TOKEN (the v1 API needs both, sent as x-rwgps-* headers), or harvest a token with 'auth-tokens --user-email <e> --user-password <pw>'.

troubleshoots.symptom: List commands return only some of my routes
troubleshoots.fix: The v1 API returns only assets you own; routes shared with you must be copied into your account first.
Run 'sync' first so track_points are mirrored locally, or pass --native to stream Ride with GPS's own file render.
Rate limits are undocumented; the CLI backs off automatically on 429 and retries — let it finish.

narrative.when_to_use: Use this CLI to bulk-export Ride with GPS routes and trips to files, mirror a library offline and query it without the API, roll up training volume and personal records from logged rides, track per-bike gear mileage and maintenance, and script route/event/POI operations. It is the right tool when a task is about your existing library and ride history rather than building or navigating a new route.
anti_triggers: Do not use this CLI for live turn-by-turn navigation — use the Ride with GPS mobile app.
Do not use this CLI to draw or build a new route on a map — use the web route planner.
Do not use this CLI to record a ride from GPS in real time — use the mobile app.
Do not use this CLI for the social activity feed or following other riders.

recipes.title: Bulk-export the whole route library to GPX for your head unit
recipes.explanation: Writes one GPX file per route (with cue sheets) from the local mirror — ready to side-load onto a Garmin or Wahoo.

recipes.title: Get an event's routes onto your bike computer
recipes.explanation: Pulls the routes attached to an organized ride and writes them as files for your head unit.

recipes.title: Find and remove duplicate routes
recipes.explanation: Clusters near-identical routes within 100m and deletes the extras (omit --apply to preview).

recipes.title: Check which bikes are due for service
recipes.explanation: Flags bikes past 4000km of accumulated trip mileage.

recipes.title: Agent: pull a route's cue sheet with narrow fields
recipes.explanation: Route detail is deeply nested and large; --select narrows to cue-sheet names and distances so an agent does not parse tens of KB of track points.

recipes.title: Agent: this year's training totals
recipes.explanation: Returns structured yearly distance/elevation/time totals for downstream reasoning.

trigger_phrases: export my ridewithgps routes to my garmin
get this event's routes on my wahoo
bulk export gpx from ride with gps
how many miles on my bike
